title: Removal and inspection of VTC hydraulic valves

blocks.text: Removal and inspection of VTC hydraulic valves
Disconnect the coupler (A). Remove the VTC hydraulic valve (B).

items: Take care to prevent foreign matter from adhering to the valve hole in the cylinder head when the VTC hydraulic valve is removed.

blocks.text: Installation is the reverse of the removal procedure.

items: O-rings shall be replaced upon disassembly.

When installing, the sleeve surface shall be cleaned with oil wash and dried with compressed air, and engine oil shall be applied before assembly to ensure that there is no foreign matter such as dust adhering to the sleeve surface.
Do not use rags or other materials during the work.

items.text: O-rings shall be replaced upon disassembly.
O-rings shall be replaced upon disassembly.

When installing, the sleeve surface shall be cleaned with oil wash and dried with compressed air, and engine oil shall be applied before assembly to ensure that there is no foreign matter such as dust adhering to the sleeve surface.

Do not use rags or other materials during the work.
Do not use rags or other materials during the work.

blocks.text: Check each port of the VTC hydraulic valve to make sure that there are no foreign objects, etc., entrapped in the valve. Ensure that the valve (A) is at least 5.0 mm from the tip.

VTC hydraulic valve Connect the (+) terminal of the 12V battery to the No. 1 terminal of the coupler and the (-) terminal of the 12V battery to the No. 2 terminal, and check that the valve (A) is at 3.4 mm or less.
